content.text: How can I protect an endpoint to ensure the user has provided a valid OAuth access token before executing the request handler?
If the external OAuth server fails or times out during token validation, what specific status code and error format will the client receive?
In case of an exception during resource validation (like a network failure), the function catches it, sets the response to a 400 Bad Request, and returns a standardized JSON error body.
This makes sense; relying on centralized token validation simplifies endpoint security.
